intro: The clearest split between Google Maps Platform and OpenCage is vendor lock-in: Google Maps Platform is rated medium (Maps APIs, swap possible but costly), while OpenCage is rated high (OSM-based, easy to swap). Google Maps Platform is Google's mapping APIs for web and mobile with geocoding, directions, Street View, and Places; it fits best for apps. OpenCage is Geocoding API aggregating OpenStreetMap and other sources with batch geocoding and GDPR compliance; it fits best for apps. Pick Google Maps Platform for apps; pick OpenCage for apps. Google Maps Platform is not for simple projects. OpenCage is not for simple projects. Honest trade-offs: Google Maps Platform is weak for apps without location features, and OpenCage is weak for apps without location features — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Maps / Geolocation / Routing umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Google Maps Platform is for apps; OpenCage is for apps; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Google Maps Platform if medium lock-in (Maps APIs, swap possible but costly) is acceptable and you need apps.
verdict_b: Choose OpenCage if high lock-in (OSM-based, easy to swap) is acceptable and you need apps.

faq.q: Is Google Maps Platform cheaper than OpenCage?
faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Google Maps Platform uses subscription pricing with no free tier published. OpenCage uses subscription pricing starting at 0.15.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Google Maps Platform to OpenCage?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: OpenCage is rated high lock-in (OSM-based, easy to swap). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Google Maps Platform scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for OpenCage). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is OpenCage a good alternative to Google Maps Platform?
faq.a: OpenCage sits in the same category as Google Maps Platform, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. OpenCage is best fit for apps. Note: OpenCage is not for simple projects.
